diagrams.title: MongoDB Architecture Overview
diagrams.description: Shows the hierarchical structure from cluster to documents
elements: Cluster → Shard → Replica Set → Database → Collection → Document → Fields
Shows how data flows from top-level cluster to individual document fields
diagrams.purpose: Understanding MongoDB's data organization hierarchy and architecture

diagrams.title: Document Structure Example
diagrams.description: Visual representation of a MongoDB document in BSON format
elements: Customer document with _id, name, email, address (embedded), orders (array)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how complex data is represented in MongoDB documents

diagrams.title: Sharding Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how data is distributed across multiple shards
elements: MongoS (router), Config servers (metadata), Shard servers (data chunks)
Shard key selection and chunk distribution
diagrams.purpose: Understanding horizontal scaling and data distribution in MongoDB

diagrams.title: Replica Set Architecture
diagrams.description: Visual representation of replica set with primary and secondary nodes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data redundancy and high availability in MongoDB

diagrams.title: Aggregation Pipeline Flow
diagrams.description: Shows data processing through pipeline stages
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data transformation and analysis in aggregation framework
